articles.title: Stephen Lewis, Canadian politician and social activist, dies aged 88
articles.description:
Lewis was the father of Avi Lewis, who was elected leader of the progressive New Democratic party one day before his father died

Stephen Lewis, the Canadian diplomat, politician and human rights advocate, who spent decades tirelessly working to focus global attention on the HIV/Aids epidemic, has died of cancer.

Lewis, who served as the Canadian ambassador to the United Nations, as well as the head of Ontario’s New Democratic party (NDP), was 88.

articles.title: ‘God squad’ waives endangered species law to allow US drilling in Gulf of Mexico
articles.description:
Critics say exemption for fossil fuels exploits White House’s ‘self-made gas crisis’, and could doom the rare Rice’s whale

articles.title: Demand for hydropower surges as Trump clamps down on clean energy
articles.description:
Home to one of the world’s largest deposits of freshwater, the Great Lakes region will soon host next-generation generators – just as prices are being hiked across the US

Submersible hydroelectric technology deployed across the Great Lakes could become a key cog in clean energy efforts, supporters say, amid surging electricity demand and costs.

Home to one of the largest deposits of freshwater on the planet, the Great Lakes region has on its shores some of the largest cities in No

articles.title: Two Women review – sex comedy remake is French-Canadian answer to Confessions of a Window Cleaner
articles.description:
This tale of a pair of women who tire of their partners and arrange sex with strangers is not sexy enough for erotica and not real enough to be drama

Chloé Robichaud’s new film is a remake of the 1970 French-Canadian sex comedy of the same name (the French title is Deux Femmes en Or) and it hasn’t travelled well: silly, clumsy and dated.

Florence (Karine Gonthier-Hyndman) and Violette (Laurence Leboeuf) are two unsatisfied women who live next door to each other in a bland suburban

articles.title: Sad faces all round as Bolivia’s clowns protest over decree threatening their livelihoods
articles.description:
Clowns in Bolivia are upset by mandate that stops schools hosting events from which they earn a living

Dozens of clowns have marched through the streets of Bolivia’s capital to protest against a government decree that limits extracurricular activities in schools, threatening their livelihoods.

Wearing full face paint and their signature red noses, the clowns gathered on Monday in front of the ministry of education in La Paz to oppose a decree published in February. The new mandate

articles.title: At least 70 people killed and 30 injured in Haiti gang attack
articles.description:
Nearly 6,000 people forced to flee, human rights group says, as it criticises ‘abandonment’ from authorities

At least 70 people have been killed and 30 injured during an attack in Haiti’s breadbasket Artibonite region, significantly more than official estimates, a human rights group has said.

Police initially reported 16 dead and 10 injured, while a preliminary report from civil protection authorities suggested 17 had died and 19 were wounded.

articles.description:
US president says he has ‘no problem’ with countries sending oil to Cuba, in potential lifeline to island nation

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

Donald Trump appears to have relaxed his blockade on fuel-starved Cuba after a Russian tanker reached the Caribbean island carrying 100,000 tonnes of crude.

Russia’s tra

articles.title: Night Stage review – public sex enthusiasm the key to extravagant and subversive erotic thriller
articles.description:
In this fantasy drama from Brazil, a gay actor and a closeted politician discover a mutual passion for the same fetish

Here is an erotic thriller from Brazil that finally suspends realism and plausibility to keep its eroticism in play right up to the startling final moments; there is an unexpected strain of the bizarre and soap-operatic.

Matias (Gabriel Faryas) is a handsome young actor in a stage company specialising in dance and physical theatre. Jealous of his friend and fellow

articles.title: Somali Government Topples State Leader as Electoral Dispute Escalates
articles.description: Crisis Group expert Omar Mahmood assesses Somalia’s standoff over polls after another alarming twist in Southwest State

articles.title: The Iran War One Month in: Any Way out?
articles.description: In this online event, Crisis Group and leading external experts took stock of the Middle East war so far, and examine its potential military and diplomatic trajectories.

articles.title: El paradigma venezolano: ¿Es la intervención modelo de Trump un espejismo?
articles.description: La sorprendente captura por parte de EE. UU. del presidente venezolano Nicolás Maduro y su esposa para trasladarlos a una prisión estadounidense ha envalentonado a la administración Trump a desplegar su poderío militar en otros lugares. Sin embargo, en Venezuela las consecuencias a mediano y largo plazo de la acción estadounidense siguen siendo inciertas.

articles.title: The Iran War Has Escaped Its Authors
articles.description: Escalation is already shattering Washington’s illusion of control.

articles.title: The Houthis Enter the Middle East War: What Comes Next?
articles.description: With the Middle East war into its second month, the Iran-aligned Houthis in Yemen have joined the fray with an attack on Israel, raising questions about what they may do now. In this Q&A, Crisis Group expert Ahmed Nagi explores factors informing the group’s calculations.

articles.title: A Hormuz Initiative to Protect Global Food Security
articles.description: The Middle East conflict threatens global food security. The International Crisis Group and prominent individuals call for an initiative, modelled on the 2022 Black Sea grain deal, to ease the transit of food, fertiliser and related intermediate materials through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Le Tchad rejoint la nouvelle force engagée dans la lutte contre les gangs en Haïti
articles.description: Les experts de Crisis Group Fulbert Ngodji et Diego Da Rin analysent les raisons ayant motivé la décision de N’Djamena et les chances de succès de cette nouvelle mission.

articles.title: On Our Radar
articles.description: On Our Radar scans conflicts and crises around the globe every week and features some of the hotspots Crisis Group’s analysts are closely watching. Whether an under-reported trend or a headline-grabbing development, our field experts explain why it matters or what should be done.

articles.title: The Venezuela Paradigm: Is Trump’s Model Intervention a Mirage?
articles.description: The startling U.S. seizure of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro and his wife for transfer to a U.S. jail has emboldened the Trump administration to flex its military muscle elsewhere. But in Venezuela itself the medium- and long-term consequences of the U.S. action remain uncertain.

articles.title: From Bad to Disastrous: Will Trump Escalate in Iran?
articles.description: This week on Hold Your Fire!, Richard talks with Crisis Group experts Ali Vaez, Yasmine Farouk, Mairav Zonszein, Stephen Pomper and Michael Wahid Hanna about where the U.S.-Israel war with Iran stands, one month in, and what might be next.

articles.title: At least 16 killed and thousands displaced by gang attack in rural Haiti
articles.description: The attack happened in the Artibonite region, where members of the Gran Grif gang have targeted locals in the past.

articles.title: Judge rules Trump unlawfully terminated legal status of migrants who used US entry app
articles.description: Thousands of people who entered the US through a Biden-era parole programme using the CBP One entry app will have their status restored.

articles.title: Russian oil tanker docks in Cuba ending near-total blockade
articles.description: A Russian oil tanker has docked in Cuba, providing temporary relief after months of shortages.

articles.title: Mexico demands answers after another migrant dies in ICE custody
articles.description: President Sheinbaum vows to take action after the death of a Mexican last week at a California facility.

articles.title: Trump Says U.S. Military Campaign to Wind Down and Plans National Address
articles.description: “We will be leaving very soon,” the president told reporters in the Oval Office. A few hours later, the White House said Mr. Trump would provide “an important update on Iran” on Wednesday evening.

articles.title: Putin’s Internet Blackout: A Chaotic Drive to Cut Off Russians From the World
articles.description: With new outages and blockages, President Vladimir V. Putin is taking his boldest steps yet to control Russians’ communications.

articles.title: Europe Has a ‘Guns vs. Butter’ Problem. War in Iran Makes It Worse.
articles.description: After decades of prioritizing domestic over military spending, the continent’s leaders are trying to pivot. That is straining national budgets and could anger voters.

articles.title: King Charles to Visit U.S. as Tensions Rise Between Trump and Britain
articles.description: President Trump has fired criticism at America’s longtime ally over the war in Iran, but there are hopes that the royal visit will help shore up relations.

articles.title: First Canadian Astronaut Will Travel to the Moon Amid Fraying U.S.-Canada Relations
articles.description: Canada will send its first astronaut to the moon on a joint mission with the United States, but back on Earth, the relationship between the two countries is fraying.

articles.title: U.S. Assures Colombian President Petro He Does Not Face Charges Right Now
articles.description: The Justice Department is examining Gustavo Petro for possible ties to drug traffickers. U.S. officials have told him that for now he does not face criminal charges arising from the probes.

articles.title: These Two Countries With Social Media Bans Aren’t Happy With Google and Meta
articles.description: Indonesia says the two companies are violating its law that bars children under 16 from the social media. Australia has similar concerns.

articles.title: U.S. Senators Press Taiwan to Raise Military Spending, as China Protests
articles.description: Four visiting senators urged Taiwan to break an impasse over a $40 billion budget proposal, highlighting concerns in Washington about the threat from China.

articles.title: How the U.K.’s Leader Decides Which U.S. Bombers to Let Fly Against Iran
articles.description: As the United States expands its armada of warplanes on British soil, Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he is trying to defend Britain’s interests while keeping the country out of war.

articles.title: A Cat-and-Mouse Game of Russian Internet Restrictions and Evasion
articles.description: As the Kremlin spends heavily on censorship technology, Russians are scrambling to find new ways to circumvent the limits.

articles.title: When Will Japan’s Cherry Blossoms Bloom? A.I. Can Help Answer That
articles.description: Experts use artificial intelligence to analyze data, plus thousands of crowdsourced photos, to forecast the prized flowers, which are a multibillion-dollar attraction.

articles.title: He Led Congo for 18 Years. Now, Joseph Kabila Is a Hunted Man.
articles.description: Joseph Kabila, the former president, faces the death penalty after the government convicted him of treason last year. He says the charges are bogus.

articles.title: Israel Passes Law to Hang Palestinians Convicted of Deadly Attacks
articles.description: Experts say the law was written in such a way as to ensure that it was unlikely to ever apply to Jewish extremists who commit similar crimes.

articles.title: Where Million-Dollar Coastal Homes Glitter Near Metal Shacks
articles.description: A shortage of affordable housing in the coastal city in South Africa has forced many people to live far outside the city center, while tourists occupy prime real estate.

articles.title: The Sailors Stranded in the Persian Gulf
articles.description: Thousands of civilian sailors have been stranded for more than a month in waters surrounded by a conflict zone because of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

articles.title: In UK Prisons, Drones Fly In Contraband ‘As if by Uber Eats’
articles.description: Decades-old prison buildings were designed to be secure from the ground but not the air. Experts say that makes a lucrative smuggling trade hard to tackle.

articles.title: Cape Town’s Housing Problem
articles.description: The rise of tourist rentals in Cape Town is driving up housing costs, deepening the inequality in the South African city that’s a legacy of apartheid. Our reporter John Eligon explores a multihour commute taken by workers who are priced out of the city.

articles.title: Trump Says U.S. Will Be Out of Iran Within Two to Three Weeks
articles.description: The White House said the president would address the nation about Iran on Wednesday evening.

articles.title: Israel Strikes Tehran Pharmaceutical Plant, Claiming Military Link
articles.description: Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, said on Tuesday that Israel was “openly and unashamedly bombing pharmaceutical companies.”

articles.title: Narges Mohammadi Denied Treatment after Heart Attack in Iranian Prison, Husband Says
Cómo la amenaza de Rusia impulsa a Alemania a romper un tabú tras la era nazi para crear la fuerza militar más poderosa de Europa
articles.description: En la primera mitad del siglo XX, Alemania aterrorizó a Europa, pero ahora sus vecinos europeos reciben con beneplácito la renaciente militarización alemana.

articles.title: "MeToo Colombia": la creciente ola de denuncias de acoso sexual a mujeres periodistas que sacude a los grandes medios
articles.description: BBC Mundo habló con las periodistas detrás de la iniciativa #YoTeCreoColega, que ya ha recibido decenas de denuncias.

articles.title: Trump les dice a Reino Unido y a otros países que "vayan a tomar su propio petróleo" al estrecho de Ormuz
articles.description: El presidente estadounidense mostró su frustración por la falta de apoyo internacional en el conflicto con Irán, mientras el cierre del paso marítimo dispara los precios del crudo.

articles.title: Cómo Santa Cruz se convirtió en un centro logístico para el narco uruguayo Sebastián Marset y el crimen transnacional desde Bolivia
articles.description: Desde el Primer Cartel Uruguayo hasta el Primer Comando de la Capital de Brasil, miembros de varios grupos delictivos coordinan sus acciones desde el oriente boliviano.

articles.title: Artemis II: recursos naturales valiosos y otras 4 razones por las cuales la NASA quiere volver a la Luna después de 50 años
articles.description: Desde la carrera espacial con China hasta los descubrimientos lunares, Estados Unidos está invirtiendo tiempo, esfuerzo y dinero para llegar a la Luna... y más allá.

articles.title: Qué significa que el gobierno de México se sume a la demanda contra ICE por muertes en un centro de detención de EE.UU.
articles.description: El anuncio ocurre después de la muerte de José Guadalupe Ramos-Solano, de 52 años y originario de Guanajuato, quien se encontraba detenido en el centro de procesamiento de Adelanto, en California. Su caso eleva a 14 el saldo de mexicanos fallecidos en el contexto de la ofensiva migratoria del segundo mandato de Trump.

articles.title: Mi ex dijo que mi futuro estaría lleno de tristeza y soledad, y luego mató a nuestros hijos. Pero usé esa misma frase para mantenerme en pie
articles.description: La agente de policía brasileña Amanda Souza recuerda el doble homicidio que se cobró la vida de sus dos hijos, de 9 y 12 años, y comenta un crimen similar ocurrido también en Brasil en febrero.

articles.title: Qué se sabe del derrame de petróleo que ha contaminado cientos de kilómetros de costa en el Golfo de México
articles.description: Las autoridades mexicanas han recogido más de 800 toneladas de residuos e investigan el origen del derrame, mientras organizaciones ambientalistas cuestionan la versión oficial.

articles.title: Qué tanto aliviará la crisis energética de Cuba la llegada de un buque ruso con petróleo
articles.description: Analizamos cuáles son las implicaciones de la llegada de petróleo ruso para Cuba, sumida en una grave crisis económica.

articles.title: "O te matan o te matas": qué se sabe del Rancho Izaguirre un año después del hallazgo en México del lugar de "reclutamiento y exterminio" del cartel CJNG
articles.description: El hallazgo de un campo de reclutamiento del Cartel Jalisco hace un año causó alarma en México y el mundo sobre el nivel de violencia que hay en lugares así. Una periodista habló con testigos que sobrevivieron o escaparon a ese sitio.

articles.title: Qué medidas están tomando los gobiernos para aliviar el aumento de los precios de los combustibles en América Latina y el mundo
articles.description: Analizamos qué están haciendo algunos países para enfrentar la crisis inflacionaria derivada de la guerra en Medio Oriente.

articles.title: Las repartidoras de yogur que combaten la soledad en Japón, uno de los países más envejecidos del mundo
articles.description: A medida que la soledad se profundiza en una de las naciones que envejece más rápidamente del mundo, una red de mujeres que entregan bebidas lácteas probióticas se ha convertido en una fuente vital de rutina, conexión y cuidado.

articles.title: Israel aprueba una ley para aplicar la pena capital a los palestinos condenados por realizar ataques "terroristas" mortales
articles.description: La nueva ley, aprobada el lunes, fue impulsada con firmeza por la extrema derecha y por el ministro de Seguridad, Itamar Ben-Gvir.

articles.title: Trump está librando contra Irán una guerra basada en el instinto y no está funcionando
articles.description: Mientras que el presidente de Estados Unidos, Donald Trump, se muestra poco preciso sobre sus objetivos militares en Irán, el primer ministro de Israel, Benjamin Netanyahu parece tener un plan más claro.

articles.title: "Es el mejor regalo de mi vida": Céline Dion anuncia su vuelta a los escenarios tras ser diagnosticada con el "síndrome de la persona rígida"
articles.description: La cantante canadiense no ofrece un espectáculo propio desde 2020, cuando su gira Courage se vio interrumpida por el estallido de la pandemia de covid-19.

articles.title: Trump dice que EE.UU. podría apoderarse "fácilmente" de la isla de Jark, el centro neurálgico de la industria petrolera de Irán
articles.description: Tomar control de la isla no sólo significaría un golpe a las exportaciones de petróleo de Irán, sino serviría de plataforma para ataques más pronunciados en el territorio iraní.

articles.title: Un petrolero de Rusia llega a Cuba con el permiso de Trump en medio del bloqueo de EE.UU. a la isla
articles.description: El carguero llegó a aguas cubanas apenas unas horas después de que Donald Trump dijera que no tenía "ningún problema" con su llegada.

articles.title: Así será el recorrido de Artemis 2 hacia la cara oculta de la Luna: el reto de explorar lo que nadie ha visto
articles.description: Cuatro astronautas sobrevolarán zonas inexploradas de la superficie lunar. El vuelo, que prioriza la seguridad de la tripulación, usa una trayectoria ‘boomerang’ capaz de devolver la nave a la Tierra sin usar sus motores

articles.title: Lori Glaze, administradora asociada de la NASA: “Es un vuelo de prueba. Muchos sistemas críticos se ensayarán por primera vez”
articles.description: Una de las máximas responsables de la agencia espacial explica a EL PAÍS los grandes hitos de la primera misión tripulada a la Luna en más de 50 años, y de las posibilidades de abortarla si hay problemas graves

articles.title: La NASA explica cómo salvarles la vida a los astronautas que viajan a la Luna
articles.description: Expertos de la misión Artemis 2 exponen a EL PAÍS todas las posibilidades de escape para la tripulación en caso de fallos graves. Todo está preparado para lanzar este miércoles

articles.title: La ciudadanía por nacimiento llega al Supremo de Estados Unidos, un caso que definirá el futuro del país
articles.description: El alto tribunal escucha este miércoles los argumentos sobre la legalidad de una orden dictada por Trump que busca impedir que todos los niños nacidos en el país obtengan la nacionalidad

articles.title: El vértigo americano: a la luna y al abismo, al mismo tiempo
articles.description: Espero con ansiedad el viaje del Artemis 2. Pero espero aún con mayor urgencia que, frente al autoritarismo de Trump, la democracia estadounidense se reafirme en las elecciones de medio término de noviembre

articles.title: Censo travado e políticas comprometidas
articles.description:
Nos dias 19 e 20 de março, participei como uma das palestrantes convidadas do 1º Simpósio do Grupo de Estudos em População e Desenvolvimento Aurélia Castiglioni no Instituto Federal do Espírito Santo (Ifes). A professora homenageada, Aurélia, estava presente e foi bonito ver o carinho e a admiração de alunos e colegas. Esse reconhecimento é especialmente significativo em um ambiente em que todos sabemos das dificuldades da vida acadêmica.

articles.title: La mutación del poder en Venezuela
articles.description:
La reconfiguración del régimen de Delcy Rodríguez no anuncia su caída, sino su adaptación: un nuevo orden en el que la estabilidad operativa desplaza a la legitimidad democrática. Por décadas, el análisis de Venezuela ha oscilado entre dos premisas recurrentes: la expectativa de un colapso inminente y la ilusión de una transición democrática inevitable. Hoy, […]

articles.description:
Los venezolanos transitamos, desde el pasado 3 de enero, un tortuoso camino hacia la transición: de la dictadura a la democracia; de la sumisión a intereses bastardos, a la recuperación de nuestra soberanía; del Estado forajido a la República. Se trata de un camino lleno de dificultades y riesgos, que puede conducirnos a alcanzar esos […]

articles.description:
María Corina Machado ha anunciado que regresará a Venezuela “en los próximos días” o “en pocas semanas”. articles.description: The death toll from a brutal gang attack in Haiti is much higher than initially thought, according to a human rights group. At least 70 people were killed and 30 more injured in a brutal gang attack in Haiti's Artibonite region, a human rights organization sa…

articles.title: White House defends prayer for ‘violence’ after Pope says God doesn’t listen to ‘those who wage war’
articles.description: Pope Leo said Jesus was a ‘King of Peace who rejects war, whomno one can use to justify war’.

articles.title: The war in Iran: Key takeaways from Al Jazeera’s interview with Marco Rubio
articles.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio spoke to Al Jazeera’s Hashem Ahelbarra about Iran war objectives.

articles.title: Human rights group says at least 70 killed in weekend attack in Haiti's Artibonite region
articles.description: At least 70 people were killed and 30 injured during an attack in Haiti's breadbasket Artibonite region, a human rights ‌group said on Monday, significantly higher than official estimates.

articles.title: WATCH: Investigation underway after Army helicopters fly to Kid Rock's house
articles.description: An investigation is underway after two Army Apache helicopters were seen flying close to Kid Rock’s home in Nashville over the weekend.

articles.title: Rubio denies US punitive actions, blames Cuba for economic failures
articles.description: In exclusive interview with Al Jazeera, the US secretary of state downplays US role in the island nation's fuel crisis.

articles.title: U.S. could exempt oil industry from protecting Gulf animals, for 'national security'
articles.description: National security has never been used to call a meeting of the "God Squad." But other federal agencies have been citing the "energy emergency" to avoid rules meant to protect endangered animals.

articles.title: Trump Blinks As Russia Defies U.S. Blockade, Delivers Oil To Cuba
articles.description: Asked about Trump’s decision to allow the Russian oil tanker and not ones from other nations, White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt called it "a decision that will continue to be made on a case-by-case basis.”

articles.title: US reopens embassy in Venezuela after seizing Nicolas Maduro
articles.description: The State Department said it had formally reopened its embassy in Caracas after seven years. The US seized President Nicolas Maduro in a military raid in January and has since reestablished diplomatic ties. The US State Deparment on Monday said it had formall…

articles.title: Trump Convenes “God Squad” Committee in Move That Could Annihilate Whale Species
articles.description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth cited national security when requesting exemptions to endangered species protections.

articles.title: NYT accuses Pentagon of flouting judge's order blocking its press access policy
articles.description: The New York Times says the Defense Department flouted a court order blocking it from enforcing a policy limiting press access to the Pentagon.

articles.title: New York Times says Pentagon is ignoring court order on press access
articles.description: A New York Times attorney claims the Pentagon has flouted a court order blocking it from enforcing a policy limiting news reporters' access to the Defense Department's headquarters.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup will be held amid ‘human rights crisis’ in the US: Amnesty
articles.description: Rights group says football tournament in Canada, Mexico and US poses risks for fans from local communities and abroad.

articles.title: Europe slams Israel over death penalty bill and freedom of religion
articles.description: European governments warned Israel that the proposed expansion of the death penalty and restrictions on religious access risk undermining democratic principles.

articles.title: Bolivian Clowns Protest New Government Decree

articles.title: 14 ICE detainees have died so far in 2026
articles.description: In 2026, 14 people have died in ICE custody, including a Mexican man who was found unresponsive last week at a facility in Los Angeles.

articles.title: Chile's president vows tougher school security following recent stabbing and firearms incidents
articles.description: Chilean President José Antonio Kast says his government will tighten school security after two recent incidents involving weapons and amid growing concern about school violence

articles.title: 'This is bigger than football' - DR Congo bid to end 52-year World Cup wait
articles.description: DR Congo face Jamaica in a World Cup play-off, hoping to reach the tournament for the first time since 1974, a campaign memorable for the wrong reasons.

articles.title: Chicago’s Brandon Johnson blasts 'assaults against immigrants' as slain Sheridan Gorman is laid to rest
articles.description: Mayor Brandon Johnson defended immigrant policies at a protest as Sheridan Gorman's family laid her to rest, vowing to fight for justice in New York.

articles.title: WTO faces new reality of global trade
articles.description: The World Trade Organization's 14th ministerial conference wrapped up in the wee hours Monday with no results on the key issues of agriculture and much-needed reform of the organisation.

articles.title: Trump wants to ‘take Iran’s oil’: Can he, and what would that mean?
articles.description: Trump said US forces could seize Iran's oil export hub at Kharg Island as the war enters its second month.

articles.title: Communists, Democrats use #NoKings rally to call for May Day strike: 'Shut it down'
articles.description: Communists chanted for revolution and May Day strikes from New York to Minnesota, as the "No Kings" movement sees a growing ideological shift to more radical rhetoric
